old_message_clean:
ApiUpload: Return file description text from stash responses

Why:
- SpecialUpload needs a new way to generate file page text as it's being
changed to perform uploads in JS in order to support chunked uploads.

What:
- Add 'license', 'copystatus' and 'source' parameters to action=upload.
- Return 'text' in stash responses returned by ApiUpload.
- Passed as 'text' during finalization so the upload's wikitext
matches what SpecialUpload POST does. This is also needed so
extensions that rely on the getInitialPageText hook will continue to
work.
new_message_clean:
ApiUpload: Return file description text from stash responses

Why:
- SpecialUpload needs a new way to generate file page text as it's being
changed to perform uploads in JS in order to support chunked uploads.

What:
- Add 'license', 'copystatus' and 'source' parameters to action=upload.
- Return 'text' in stash responses returned by ApiUpload.
- Passed as 'text' during finalization so the upload's wikitext
matches what SpecialUpload POST does. This is also needed so
extensions that rely on the getInitialPageText hook will continue to
work.
- Set non-fatal 'upload-textwithstash' warning when 'text' is passed
alongside 'stash'.
- 'text' is ignored in this context and we should signal to API
callers that this is improper usage of the API.

old_message_clean:
ApiUpload: Return file description text from stash responses

Why:
- SpecialUpload needs a new way to generate file page text as it's being
changed to perform uploads in JS in order to support chunked uploads.

What:
- Add 'license', 'copystatus' and 'source' parameters to action=upload.
- Return 'text' in stash responses returned by ApiUpload.
- Passed as 'text' during finalization so the upload's wikitext
matches what SpecialUpload POST does. This is also needed so
extensions that rely on the getInitialPageText hook will continue to
work.
- Set non-fatal 'upload-textwithstash' warning when 'text' is passed
alongside 'stash'.
- 'text' is ignored in this context and we should signal to API

new_message_clean:
ApiUpload: Generate 'text' when 'autotext' is true

Why:
- SpecialUpload needs a new way to generate file page text as it's being
changed to perform uploads in JS in order to support chunked uploads.

What:
- Add new optional 'autotext', 'license', 'copystatus' and 'source'
parameters to action=upload.
- Set 'text' to a generated value when 'autotext' is true in ApiUpload.
- This exists to allow the upload's wikitext to match what
SpecialUpload POST creates. This is also needed so extensions that
rely on the getInitialPageText hook will continue to work.
- Set non-fatal 'upload-textwithstash' warning when 'text' is passed
alongside 'stash'.
- 'text' is ignored in this context and we should signal to API
callers that this is improper usage of the API.
- Set non-fatal 'upload-autotextwithstash' warning when 'autotext' is
passed alongside 'stash'.

old_message_clean: Rename 'Public knowledge' to 'LLM Knowledge' for accurate alignment
new_message_clean:
Rename 'Public knowledge' to 'LLM Knowledge' for accurate alignment
The label "Public knowledge" was misleading — it refers specifically to
the LLM's built-in parametric knowledge, not publicly available
information in general. Rename it to "LLM Knowledge" across all
user-facing strings, i18n messages, and documentation.

Internal API parameter names (usepublicknowledge, publicknowledge) are
unchanged to avoid breaking API compatibility.

Notify users of multi-revision undos and reverts, and EditResult-related cleanup

It is possible to revert multiple users, but currently no user will be notified for that case. This commit notifies all users whose edits were reverted. It also cleans up Echo hooks, which can be simplified/changed as a result of EditResult being added to the core.

new_message_clean:
Notify users of multi-revision reverts; EditResult-related cleanup

Goals:
* Clean up two Echo hooks that can be simplified thanks to the
introduction of EditResult in core. This also gets rid of a
pesky static variable in EchoHooks.php.
* Notify all reverted users in multi-edit reverts.
* Correctly inform the user whether a single or multiple edits were
reverted.
* Notify users about manual reverts.

This change set:

* Integrates new core re-auth/priv-elevation functionality into
EditPage, specifically for editing site/user js/css.

* Adds a new data stashing trait. Since we want to re-auth after
a form save attempt, we need to store any relevant user-submitted
data in the session. We persist this data with a short TTL so it
doesn't linger forever after bad re-auth attempts or page reloads.
Ultimately, this code could probably live somewhere else in core,
but it should be ok for now as a trait, for this use-case.

new_message_clean:
tdf#167632 Add additional columns when converting text2table

Add an "Add [n] Columns" spin button to the Convert Text to Table
dialog, letting users choose how many empty columns (0-20) to append
when converting text to a table.

Instead of inserting separator characters into the source text (which
left those characters behind in the document when the conversion was
reverted), pass the requested column count down to SwNodes::TextToTable
and raise nMaxBoxes accordingly. lcl_BalanceTable() then creates the
extra empty boxes via InsBoxen() without touching the text, and
registers them with the undo object, so reverting the conversion
restores exactly the originally selected text.

old_message_clean:
Fix window sizing on macOS when using Qt6

macOS has this goofy 2 tier system of logical pixels for windows and views.
But they are backed by image buffers that are 2x2 physical pixels for each
logical pixel.

Qt6 apparently handles this differently than the default macOS vcl plugin
so we need to have different code for window sizing when the Qt6 plugin is
used on macOS.

tdf#172113 sw ms import: consistent linespacing gap height

I guess they probably used to be consistent before LO 7.1.
After 7.1, only the last line still had a gap determined by
the largest portion on the line.
The other lines were determined primarily
by the largest TextPortion.

That was both inconsistent and not interoperable with MS formats.

So change mnHeightOfLastLine to use 7.1's GetLineSpacingBaseHeight,
now that IsUsedToCalcLineSpacingHeight
produces a much more interoperable line spacing result.

SAFETY FACTOR: very safe
It fixes MS formats and only affects new documents.

CPPUNIT_TEST_NAME=testTdf164835_nonDummyLineHeight

tdf#172233 sw: apply line spacing gap after the line, not before

This changes the default behaviour of ODT documents,
as well as fixing document layout for Microsoft formats.

Prior to this patch, the line's calculated line spacing gap
was placed above the line.

There were two problems with that:
1.) The height of the first line was ignored.
It has (always) used the mnHeightOfLastLine as its 'before gap'.
This means that the last line's height determines two gaps,
while the first line's height didn't determine any gap size.

2.) Font designers already build a gap
appropriate for clean single line spacing into the font itself.
By adding the (larger) gap above the (already large) font
it didn't create a visually appealing paragraph.

And of course, since it doesn't match Microsoft's layout,
it neither displayed native DOC/X files correctly,
nor did it nicely enable ODT -> DOCX conversions.

While not everything done 'the Microsoft way' is better,
this aspect of their line-space handling certainly is superior.

make CppunitTest_sw_ooxmlexport25 \
CPPUNIT_TEST_NAME=testTdf172233_linespacingGap
new_message_clean:
tdf#172233 sw: apply line spacing gap after the line, not before

This improves document layout for Microsoft formats.

Prior to this patch, the line's calculated line spacing gap
was placed above the line (for intra-paragraph line spacing).

There are two problems with LO's way of placing the gap above:
1.) The height of the first line is ignored.
LO uses the mnHeightOfLastLine as the first line's 'before gap'.
This means that the last line's height determines two gaps,
while the first line's height doesn't determine any gap size.

2.) Font designers already build a gap
appropriate for single line spacing into the font itself.
By adding the (larger) gap above the (already large) font
it didn't create a visually appealing paragraph.

And of course, since it doesn't match Microsoft's layout,
it didn't display native DOC/X files correctly.

While not everything done 'the Microsoft way' is better,
this aspect of their line-space handling certainly is superior.
Perhaps it should be made the default for new ODT documents too.

SAFETY FACTOR: very safe
It hides all the changes behind a compat flag.

new_message_clean:
tdf#172169 sw ms import: IsMultiPortion IsUsedToCalcLineSpacingHeight

Pure RTL text is just an SwTextPortion,
but I guess if there is anything 'different' in the paragraph
then it becomes a multi-portion SwBidiPortion.
So clearly SwBidiPortions ought to inform the line-specing gap.

Interestingly, rotated text should be treated the same as normal text
in terms of determining the line-spacing gap (according to MS Word).
It seems a bit odd to do so IMHO, but it is consistent at least.

Although our double-line Asian layout is not that closely aligned
to how Microsoft implements it, MS Word clearly does define
the line-spacing gap based on the height of double-lined text.

I assume that this applies to Rubies as well
(where again our implementation seems rather different from MS Word).

So then these should all be treated the same as normal text
in terms of determing the line-spacing gap.

SAFETY FACTOR: very safe
It fixes MS formats and only affects new documents.

This fixes a LO 7.1 regression
from commit d336e6c26012255015d3fc0caf8e7fafe14bd8f2
which caused intra-paragraph line-spacing calculations
to ignore the size of non-plain-text content.

Specifically broken for both ODT and Microsoft formats
were things like fields, content controls, and FieldMarkCheckboxes.

I took some liberties to NOT revert everything that was 'broken',
because some bits now match how Microsoft handles line spacing.
So take the 5-year-old accidental win on those
and turn the native LO implementation into an interoperable one.

So the things I change are 'revert to previous behaviour'
and the things I leave in are 'accept the current behaviour'.
There is no 'correct behaviour' because there were no
compatibility flags involved earlier.

Things that LO 7.1 accidentally fixed in terms of Microsoft content
were Tabstops, Hidden text, and Footnote/Endnote markers.

new_message_clean:
tdf#172113 sw line spacing: introduce LINE_SPACING_AS_GAP_BELOW

Currently, line spacing is a totally inconsistent
non-interoperable mess.

LO 7.1 introduced radical changes
without a compat flag, and without handling edge cases,
so inconsistencies and unpredictabilities abound.

The ODF (and DOCX) specifications say nothing
about how line-spacing should be implemented.

This LO 27.2 patch introduces a new compat flag
which will be utilized by succeeding patches.

Things which will be fixed:
1.) Consistently place the gap below the line.
Currently this is only done for the last line (by the first line).
The other lines place their gap above the line.

2.) Also use the first line to define a gap.
Currently the last line defines two gaps (above and below),
while the first line doesn't define any.

3.) Consistently define the gap height for all lines.
Currently the last line's gap depends on the tallest 'anything'
while the other other gaps are (supposedly - since LO 7.1)
determined only by TextPortions.

4.) Predictably define the gap height.
-don't give special advantage to the first portion
-don't ignore text-like content (fields, bidi, etc.)
-resolve various implementation errors

5.) Be highly interoperable with MS formats
Currently our line spacing doesn't look anything
like how MS Word shows it.

new_message_clean:
tdf#172169 sw line spacing: SwFootnotePortion != IsTextFrame

This odd bug is even seen prior to the 7.1 changes,
but it is clearly wrong given 7.1's TextPortions only...

Since the footnote marker in the text run
is not considered to be IsTextFrame or InTextGrp,
it doesn't affect either LO or MSO line spacing
and thus should not set LineSpacingBaseHeight.
[I'm using terminology from later patches here^^^]

I caught this when doing a
make CppunitTest_sw_ooxmlexport16 \
CPPUNIT_TEST_NAME=testTdf141231_arabicHebrewNumbering
where a debug/sanity check walked through the portions
and found a highest value that didn't match GetLineSpacingBaseHeight.

IIUC, on a final run of the line
(after a prior run had already determined the final line height)
it was taking that finalized line height [rInf.GetTextHeight()]
and applying it to LineSpacingBaseHeight.

SAFETY FACTOR: unsafe, but necessary/exotic
This is a real edge case, and should generally
have minimal effect in a typical, reasonable document.
This fixes a bug, not a 'previous layout choice'.

In the worst case, it could easily be put behind a compat flag.
const IDocumentSettingAccess& rIDSA
= m_pInf->GetTextFrame()->GetDoc().getIDocumentSettingAccess();

make CppunitTest_sw_ooxmlexport25 \
CPPUNIT_TEST_NAME=testTdf172169_linespacingFootnote

old_message_clean:
Fetch extension preview images in parallel

The Extensions dialog was downloading preview images one at a time inside
SearchAndParseThread, making the dialog populate slowly.

Use ImageFetchTask and dispatch one task per extension to the shared thread pool.
Search() now collects the extensions, waits for all downloads to complete,
then updates the UI in a single serialized pass under SolarMutexGuard.

I tried to follow the same pattern as GraphicFilter::ImportGraphics() in
vcl/source/filter/graphicfilter.cxx.
new_message_clean:
Fetch extension preview images in parallel

The Extensions dialog was downloading preview images one at a time
inside SearchAndParseThread, making the dialog populate slowly.

Use ImageFetchTask and dispatch one task per extension to the shared
thread pool. Search() now collects the extensions, waits for all
downloads to complete, then updates the UI in a single serialized pass
under SolarMutexGuard.

I tried to follow the same pattern as GraphicFilter::ImportGraphics() in
vcl/source/filter/graphicfilter.cxx.

old_message_clean:
tdf#172513 ko char conv: Preserve char represented by >1 UTF-16 code unit

In order to represent the (codepoint of a) Unicode character
in UTF-16, multiple UTF-16 code units may be needed.

So far, the Korean Hangul/Hanja character conversion
was assuming that each Unicode character was only
cosnisting of a single UTF-16 code unit, and splitting
into single sal_Unicode characters accordingly in
TextConversion_ko::getCharConversions.

This breaks for characters represented by multiple
code units, like "𡽜" (CJK Unified Ideograph [1],
unicode codepoint: 139100/U+21F5C) which is represented
by 2 UTF-16 code units.

As a consequence, an invalid OUString/UTF-16 string would
be passed to HangulHanjaConversionDialog::SetCurrentString
for some of the suggestions.

Fix this by no longer splitting into single sal_Unicode,
but use OUString::iterateCodePoints to split into actual
code points.

Example: When using "Tools" -> "Language" ->
"Hangul/Hanja Conversion..." for a Korean text of
"한글맞춤법", the string of all characters for potential
conversions in TextConversion_ko::getCharConversions is:
"韓漢寒限閑恨旱汗翰邯罕悍澣閒瀚桿捍閈暵鼾犴僩嫺熯嫻駻鷳扞狠僴覵瞷猂邗骭撊鷴垾憪橌㸁䛞䦘闲鹇娴汉焊闬韩仠佷傼兯哻娨嫨屽晘橺浫澖皔蓒蔊蛝螒豻貋釬銲雗鶾鷼麲㒏㛠㡾㢨㪋㯗㲦㵄䁂䂅䈨䉯䍐䍑䎯䏷䓍䓳䕿䗙䥜䦥䮧𡽜忓"

Previously, the last 3 of them ("䮧𡽜忓") would incorrectly be
split into (stdout output, showing something is wrong):
"䮧", " " (something wrong, but a valid UTF-16 string), "?" (wrong, and invalid UTF-16 string), "忓"

With the fix in place, they remain intact:
"䮧", "𡽜", "忓"

Add a corresponding test case.
While the fix is in i18npool, the new test is implemented
in editeng, where the HangulHanjaConversion class uses
the i18npool logic via a UNO service.
The test is implemented there because the component context
availabe in i18npool doesn't supply the required service.
The same test implemented there would fail like this:

An uncaught UNO exception
- com.sun.star.uno.DeploymentException: {Message: "component context fails to supply service com.sun.star.linguistic2.ConversionDictionaryList of type com.sun.star.linguistic2.XConversionDictionaryList at /home/michi/development/git/libreoffice/workdir/UnoApiHeadersTarget/offapi/normal/com/sun/star/linguistic2/ConversionDictionaryList.hpp:45", Context: @0x7fff17acecf0}

[1] https://codepoints.net/U+21F5C

- Table Properties dialog: validation in DeactivatePage that
showed an info dialog
- Navigator rename: forbidden characters list
- UNO SwXTextTable::setName: check that threw RuntimeException

Other illegal characters (".<>") are still blocked, since they
are incorrectly handled in formulas and chart data sources.

Also remove the now unused STR_WRONG_TABLENAME string.

Fix found with the help of Claude Opus.

new_message_clean:
tdf#71261: Allow spaces in writer table names

Drop the space character from the places that rejected it in
table names:

- Insert Table dialog: text filter on the name entry
- Table Properties dialog: validation in DeactivatePage that
showed an info dialog
- Navigator rename: forbidden characters list
- UNO SwXTextTable::setName: check that threw RuntimeException

Other illegal characters (".<>") are still blocked, since they
are incorrectly handled in formulas and chart data sources.

Also remove the now unused STR_WRONG_TABLENAME string.

Allowing spaces exposed that a chart linked to such a table lost
its data connection after save/reload, so fix the table name
(de)serialization on both sides of the link:

- ODF export (xmltexte.cxx) wrote draw:notify-on-update-of-ranges
wrapped in single quotes when the name contained a space; write
it unmodified instead (the attribute also allows a plain string,
not only a cellRangeAddressList).
- ODF import (xmltexti.cxx) only unquotes the legacy single-quoted
form when the value actually starts with a quote, so both old and
new documents are read correctly.
- SwChartDataProvider::convertRangeFromXML split the space separated
range list with a naive tokenizer that broke inside a single-quoted
table name; split it in a quote-aware way so the chart range still
resolves to the table (instead of falling back to the chart's
internal local-table).

Added regression tests:
- verify that the Insert Table dialog accepts spaces in the
table name
- verify that a table name with a space can be set via UNO
and is preserved
- verify that the Table Properties dialog accepts and saves
a table name containing a space
- verify that, after save/reload, the chart's
notify-on-update-of-ranges keeps the unquoted table name
- verify the convertRangeToXML/convertRangeFromXML round-trip for
a table name containing a space

Fix found with the help of Claude Opus.

Issue-ID: SDC-4829
new_message_clean:
Replace WorkspaceViewModel controller with Angular WorkspaceContainerComponent

Port the 1032-line AngularJS WorkspaceViewModel controller to a pure Angular
component (WorkspaceContainerComponent). The component is downgraded via
@angular/upgrade/static and used within the existing ui-router workspace state.

This is Phase 2 of the AngularJS removal migration (SDC-4829). The workspace
shell - sidebar menu, top bar with lifecycle buttons, version selector,
breadcrumbs, create/save/archive/restore actions - is now Angular code while
child tab states continue rendering unchanged as AngularJS/ui-router.

Key architecture (verified locally on the production Docker build via the
webseal-simulator - the same environment as the CI Selenium UI tests - for
VIEW/EDIT, CREATE and tab-switch flows, all settling per pageLoadWait):

- The child <div ui-view> is a SIBLING of <workspace-container>, NOT projected
through its <ng-content>. Projecting a ui-router ui-view through an Angular
component's content slot relocates the view anchor and hangs the page in an
infinite digest. CSS (.workspace-child-view in workspace.less) positions the
sibling into the shell content region.
- Component data flows via WorkspaceService.setComponent(), called in ALL
branches of the workspace-state resolve (existing id, CSAR, and empty/create),
not via @Input - downgradeComponent with propagateDigest:false does not
deliver inputs.
- ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush + propagateDigest:false + a single
detectChanges() in ngOnInit render the shell without an infinite CD loop.
- A thin AngularJS shim controller on the workspace state exposes the $scope
properties (component, setValidState, etc.) that AngularJS child tabs still
expect; removed in Phase 3/4.
- AngularJS services resolved lazily via $injector.get() in ngOnInit (no eager
constructor string-token injection). WorkspaceTopProgressComponent replaces
the AngularJS top-progress directive. LayoutModule provides TopNavComponent.
CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA for remaining AngularJS directives in the template.

Also adds local UI dev/verification tooling: webpack-dev-server now defaults to
a local backend with a built-in login page (catalog-ui/webpack.server.js,
start:local script), a workspace-shell Playwright spec, and Docker-backend
prerequisites in the playwright-tests README.

new_message_clean:
Replace WorkspaceViewModel controller with Angular WorkspaceContainerComponent

Port the 1032-line AngularJS WorkspaceViewModel controller to a pure Angular
component (WorkspaceContainerComponent). The component is downgraded via
@angular/upgrade/static and used within the existing ui-router workspace state.

This is Phase 2 of the AngularJS removal migration (SDC-4829). The workspace
shell - sidebar menu, top bar with lifecycle buttons, version selector,
breadcrumbs, create/certify/check-in actions - is now Angular code while child
tab states continue rendering unchanged as AngularJS/ui-router.

Key architecture (verified locally on the production Docker build via the
webseal-simulator - the same environment as the CI Selenium UI tests):

- The child <div ui-view> is a SIBLING of <workspace-container>, NOT projected
through its <ng-content>. Projecting a ui-router ui-view through an Angular
component's content slot relocates the view anchor and hangs the page in an
infinite digest. CSS (.workspace-child-view in workspace.less) positions the
sibling into the shell content region.
- Component data flows via WorkspaceService.setComponent(), called in ALL
branches of the workspace-state resolve (existing id, CSAR, and empty/create),
not via @Input - downgradeComponent with propagateDigest:false does not
deliver inputs.
- ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush + propagateDigest:false + detectChanges() in
ngOnInit and after each action render the shell without an infinite CD loop.
- The shell owns the action logic (create, changeLifecycleState,
handleChangeLifecycleState, startProgress/stopProgress) ported from the old
controller. ChangeLifecycleStateHandler is given the component as its scope so
it drives the shell loader (isLoading / isCreateProgress). The create and
lifecycle buttons are wired to these methods - previously they had no handler,
which broke the Selenium create flow (the loader never appeared and
waitForLoader timed out).
- The <sdc-loader> create/lifecycle spinners use [active] binding (not *ngIf) so
they render the loader DOM the Selenium LoaderHelper.waitForLoader() waits for.
- A thin AngularJS shim controller on the workspace state exposes the $scope
properties (component, setValidState, etc.) and delegates the action methods
(create / changeLifecycleState / save) to the shell via
WorkspaceService.containerActions, for AngularJS child tabs that still call
them via $scope inheritance; removed in a later phase.
- AngularJS services resolved lazily via $injector.get() in ngOnInit.
WorkspaceTopProgressComponent replaces the AngularJS top-progress directive.
LayoutModule provides TopNavComponent. CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA for remaining
AngularJS directives in the template.

Also adds local UI dev/verification tooling: webpack-dev-server now defaults to
a local backend with a built-in login page (catalog-ui/webpack.server.js,
start:local script), a workspace-shell Playwright spec covering VIEW/EDIT,
CREATE, the create flow (loader parity) and tab navigation, and Docker-backend
prerequisites in the playwright-tests README.

new_message_clean:
Replace WorkspaceViewModel controller with Angular WorkspaceContainerComponent

Port the 1032-line AngularJS WorkspaceViewModel controller to a pure Angular
component (WorkspaceContainerComponent). The component is downgraded via
@angular/upgrade/static and used within the existing ui-router workspace state.

This is Phase 2 of the AngularJS removal migration (SDC-4829). The workspace
shell - sidebar menu, top bar with lifecycle buttons, version selector,
breadcrumbs, create/certify/check-in actions - is now Angular code while child
tab states continue rendering unchanged as AngularJS/ui-router.

Key architecture (verified locally on the production Docker build via the
webseal-simulator - the same environment as the CI Selenium UI tests - where
the full ImportVfcUiTest.importVFC_createVF_addVFC2VF_test passes end-to-end):

- The child <div ui-view> is a SIBLING of <workspace-container>, NOT projected
through its <ng-content>. Projecting a ui-router ui-view through an Angular
component's content slot relocates the view anchor and hangs the page in an
infinite digest. CSS (.workspace-child-view in workspace.less) positions the
sibling into the shell content region.
- Component data flows via WorkspaceService.setComponent(), called in ALL
branches of the workspace-state resolve (existing id, CSAR, and empty/create),
not via @Input - downgradeComponent with propagateDigest:false does not
deliver inputs.
- ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush + propagateDigest:false + detectChanges() in
ngOnInit and after each action render the shell without an infinite CD loop.
- The shell owns the action logic (create, changeLifecycleState,
handleChangeLifecycleState, startProgress/stopProgress) ported from the old
controller. ChangeLifecycleStateHandler is given the component as its scope so
it drives the shell loader (isLoading / isCreateProgress). The create and
lifecycle buttons are wired to these methods - previously they had no handler,
which broke the Selenium create flow (the loader never appeared and
waitForLoader timed out).
- A thin AngularJS shim controller on the workspace state exposes the $scope
properties (component, setValidState, etc.) and delegates the action methods
(create / changeLifecycleState / save) to the shell via
WorkspaceService.containerActions, for AngularJS child tabs that still call
them via $scope inheritance; removed in a later phase.
- AngularJS services resolved lazily via $injector.get() in ngOnInit.
WorkspaceTopProgressComponent replaces the AngularJS top-progress directive.
LayoutModule provides TopNavComponent. CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA for remaining
AngularJS directives in the template.

Selenium UI-gate parity fixes (the migrated shell must keep the exact DOM the
Selenium page objects assert on; each broke a different step of ImportVfcUiTest):

- Loader markup: render an inline tlv-loader / data-tests-id="loader" element
(mirroring the old AngularJS <loader> directive) instead of <sdc-loader>, whose
service-driven activation rendered no element LoaderHelper.waitForLoader()
could see. Keep isCreateProgress true through the create->navigate window so
the loader does not vanish within a Selenium poll interval on a fast backend.
- Debounced ng-model commit: the General-tab form fields use ng-model-options
"{debounce:500}"; the ported Angular create() runs outside the AngularJS
digest, so a Selenium-speed fill-then-Create read the component before the
debounced writes committed (create POST without description -> backend 400 ->
loader only flashed). create() now force-commits via ngModelController
.$commitViewValue() before reading the component.
- Lifecycle-button data-tests-id is run through the same testsId transform the
old template used (e.g. 'Certify' -> 'certify'), so the Selenium locators match.
- Certify refreshes the component in place (update lifecycleState + buttons +
detectChanges) instead of $state.go(reload:true), which re-mounted the
downgraded shell with a gap where the shell container was absent from the DOM.
- The shell re-subscribes to $stateChangeSuccess (as the old controller did) to
recompute the selected sidebar item / tab title and isComposition/isDeployment
on each child-tab navigation.
- The .w-sdc-main-right-container div is kept always-present with a STATIC class
and the *ngIf="component" guard moved to an inner display:contents wrapper, so
Angular's structural-directive marker class (ng-star-inserted) and the
[class.composition]/[class.deployment] bindings no longer pollute its class
attribute. The Selenium page objects wait on the EXACT-class xpath
//div[@class='w-sdc-main-right-container'] (and the nested
//div[@class='sdc-workspace-top-bar']); an extra class made findElement match
zero nodes and time out even though the page rendered.

Also adds local UI dev/verification tooling: webpack-dev-server now defaults to
a local backend with a built-in login page (catalog-ui/webpack.server.js,
start:local script), a workspace-shell Playwright spec covering VIEW/EDIT,
CREATE, the create flow (loader parity) and tab navigation, and Docker-backend
prerequisites in the playwright-tests README.
